x-enumeration-literals.name: Full Allocation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The entire collateral value is assigned to a single obligation.
No sharing with other exposures.

Comment:
Example: A €100,000 fixed deposit fully secures Loan A only.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Partial Allocation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Only a portion of the collateral is assigned to an obligation.
The remaining value stays available for other uses.

Comment:
Example: €200,000 property → €120,000 allocated to Loan A, €80,000 unallocated or reserved.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Shared Allocation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The same collateral is simultaneously allocated to multiple obligations.
Each exposure references the same collateral with defined shares.

Comment
Example: One asset secures Loan A and Loan B at defined percentages (e.g., 50/50).

x-enumeration-literals.name: Pool-Based Allocation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Collateral is grouped into a pool and collectively secures multiple obligations.

Allocation is managed at pool level rather than individual asset level.

Comment:
Example: A securities pool secures all revolving credit facilities of a customer.
